raw_response: {"simulated_user_message":"I keep trying to paint, but I'm so drained I can't even pick up my brushes. Dad's needs are nonstop, and my partner's frustration feels like it's piling onto the weight. Grandma’s voice still echoes in my head, and I feel guilty about not doing more. How do I even find a moment to breathe?","problems_referred":["caregiver_stress","conflicts_with_partner","grief_of_loved_one"]}

parsed.reasoning: I'm here as a friend to help you navigate this tough time, and you're expressing resistance to the idea of finding moments to breathe, feeling overwhelmed by your responsibilities. Given your intent to resist, I'll use the support strategy to acknowledge your feelings without arguing. You've also mentioned the weight of your dad's needs, your partner's frustration, and the memory of your grandma, which are all contributing to your feelings of guilt and overwhelm. My entry point will be to acknowledge the weight of these responsibilities and the emotional toll they're taking on you.

parsed.final_response: It's like the weight of everything is crushing you, and even things that usually bring you comfort, like painting, feel out of reach right now. You're shouldering so much - your dad's constant needs, your partner's frustration, and the memory of your grandma. It's no wonder you're feeling drained and guilty. Yeah, it can be really tough to find those tiny moments to breathe when everything else is feeling so overwhelming. Can you think of one small thing that you've done in the past that helped you feel even a little bit more grounded, even if it was just for a moment?
